location: Washington, D.C. and San Pedro, CA
description: Congresswoman Barragan's office is hiring! We are working to hire a new cohort of interns to work this Winter/Spring. The positions include: San Pedro | District Office Interns, D.C. Office Interns. Don't miss the opportunity! Applications for the Winter/Spring internships are being accepted on a rolling basis until positions are filled.
responsibilities: Work as part of a new cohort of interns during the Winter/Spring term
Serve in either the San Pedro District Office or D.C. Office
Support office operations as an intern
qualifications: Available to work during the Winter/Spring term
Ability to work in Washington, D.C. or San Pedro, CA
how_to_apply: Apply now at www.Barragan.house.gov/internships. Applications for the Winter/Spring internships are being accepted on a rolling basis until positions are filled.
equal_opportunity: Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. All are welcome and encouraged to apply.

description:
The office of Congressman Jeff Van Drew (NJ-02) is currently accepting applications for full and part-time interns for the summer internship session. The start date would be as early as May 9th (flexible depending on availability) with a flexible end date. Interns are paid a stipend based on availability and have access to transit benefits.

The internship will offer students and young professionals invaluable experience with our nation's legislative process. Interns contribute to the daily operations of the office and are expected to act in a reliable, professional manner at all times.

Congressman Van Drew supports students requesting credit for their internship through their university.
responsibilities: Assist staff with various other tasks
qualifications: Students and young professionals
Ability to act in a reliable and professional manner
University students requesting credit supported
how_to_apply: If interested, please email a resume and cover letter to JeffVanDrewNJ@gmail.com. Please indicate in your cover letter that you're applying for a spring internship and format the files as [Full Name_Resume], [Full Name_Cover Letter].
salary_info: Stipend based on availability; transit benefits included

description: The Office of Congressman Raja Krishnamoorthi (IL-08) is seeking a highly motivated, proactive press intern to assist on an aggressive communications team. Qualified candidates will demonstrate a strong and positive work ethic, pay attention to detail, be responsive and punctual, and have solid writing and editing skills. Preferred applicants are diligent, communicative, creative, and are willing to perform and follow through on a wide variety of tasks. Hours = 9am-5pm ET Monday through Friday. Position is virtual. Start date = February; End date = May.
responsibilities: Compiling press clips by 9:30am ET every morning
Social media strategizing for increased growth
Drafting daily social media posts and ideas for Instagram, Twitter, and Facebook
Creating graphics for social media, knowledge of Photoshop or Canva preferred
Editing written materials, such as newsletters or press releases
Other duties as needed to support the communications team
qualifications: Strong and positive work ethic
Attention to detail
Responsive and punctual
Solid writing and editing skills
Diligent, communicative, and creative
Willing to perform and follow through on a wide variety of tasks
Knowledge of Photoshop or Canva preferred
how_to_apply: Please send resume, cover letter, 2 writing samples, and a graphic sample (if applicable). Send to KrishnamoorthiPressInternship@gmail.com

description:
The Office of Congressman Greg Murphy (NC-03) is currently seeking interns with our DC office for the Spring 2022 session. The internship would begin January 19th and last until May 14th.

This is a great opportunity to gain hands-on experience and knowledge over the day-to-day happenings within Congress. Additionally, interns currently enrolled in an accredited university will receive a monthly stipend.

The internship is an ideal fit for any student that is interested in politics, law or government - however, any interested student is encouraged to apply! Interns will assist the legislative staff by conducting policy research, answering phones, leading tours, and helping with administrative tasks.
qualifications: Be a current undergraduate or graduate student, or have recently graduated
Demonstrate an interest in politics, policy and/or public service
Have strong communications and writing skills
Be hard-working, dependable, detail-oriented and professional
North Carolina ties are preferred but not required
how_to_apply: Address application materials to Katonya Pettaway at Katonya.Pettaway@mail.house.gov. Applications should include a cover letter, resume, and what the individual's internship schedule may look like.
salary_info: Monthly stipend for currently enrolled students

office: House Committee on Oversight and Reform, Subcommittee on Government Operations (Democratic Staff)
description:
The Democratic staff of the House Committee on Oversight and Reform, Subcommittee on Government Operations, is seeking an energetic and self-motivated graduate student for a full-time internship for Spring 2022. The position offers the opportunity to gain substantive experience on an exciting and fast-paced Subcommittee, while participating directly in the oversight process and learning about the day-to-day operations of the House of Representatives.

The Committee on Oversight and Reform is the primary investigative committee of the U.S. House of Representatives, with jurisdiction to investigate any matter at any time. The Subcommittee on Government Operations has jurisdiction over issues related to the federal civil service, whistleblower protections, the U.S. Postal Service; government management and accounting measures; the economy, efficiency, and management of government operations and activities; government reorganization; intergovernmental affairs, including state and local governments; federal information technology security, acquisition policy, and management; and federal property.
responsibilities: Assist staff in preparing for congressional hearings
Attend various briefings and meetings with Executive Branch and private sector officials
Work closely with Committee counsel and staff
qualifications: Interest in public service
Desire to learn
Superior attention to detail
Ability to work well under pressure
Self-motivated
Excellent written and oral communication skills
Willingness to be in the office full time
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should apply to this position by emailing their resume and cover letter in one PDF titled "last name, first name, Spring 2022 GovOps" to Government.Operations@mail.house.gov with "Spring 2022 Internship Application" in the subject line. No phone calls or drop-ins please. Only applicants who are selected for an interview will receive a response.

description:
Congressman Dan Newhouse (R-WA-04) is currently seeking a paid congressional intern for the Spring 2022 term. The internship is in-person and will run from early February to late May. Dates are flexible. This internship is open to undergraduate and recent college graduates.

Intern responsibilities include answering phone calls, opening and sorting mail, greeting guests, assisting staff with administrative and legislative tasks, and producing a daily memo. Ideal candidates will have a positive attitude, strong work ethic, attention to detail, and be able to work in a fast-paced work environment. District ties will be considered however are not required.
responsibilities: Assist staff with administrative and legislative tasks
qualifications: Undergraduate student or recent college graduate
Positive attitude
Strong work ethic
Attention to detail
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
how_to_apply: Submit a resume and cover letter with the subject line 'Spring 2022 Internship' to Ben.Savercool@mail.house.gov.
